zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton於1126年之前與Richer of Tarbolton在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton于1126年之前与Richer of Tarbolton在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Richer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riquier
meanings: French: variant of   Richer   1. Compare   Rickey  .

History: François Riquet or Riquier dit Laverdure from Saint-Léger-Dubosq in Calvados, France, married Marie-Anne Renaud in Montreal, QC, in 1699. His descendants also bear an altered form of the surname Riquier, Rickey, and also the surname Laverdure .

variants.name: Ritzer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Otto.

variants.name: Richert
meanings: Anglo-Norman: An Anglo-Norman form of Richard.

variants.name: Richard
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rich
meanings: Short form of Richard . There was a medieval given name Rich(e), but it is connected only indirectly with the modern form. It is a short form of several medieval names, including not only Richard but also other, rarer names of Old French (Germanic) origin with the same first element, as, for example, Rich(i)er ‘power army’ and Richaud ‘power rule’.

variants.name: Ricker
meanings: German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’.
English: variant of both   Richer  , a cognate of 1 above, and   Rickard  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton married Richer of Tarbolton before 1126, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton a épousé Richer of Tarbolton avant 1126, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton hat vor 1126 in Scotland Richer of Tarbolton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton lei sposato Richer of Tarbolton prima del 1126, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarboltonは1126年以前にスコットランドでRicher of Tarboltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton(은)는 1126년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Richer of Tarbolton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton casou-se com Richer of Tarbolton antes 1126, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton вышла замуж за человека по имени Richer of Tarbolton перед 1126 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Richer of Tarbolton se casó con Richer of Tarbolton antes de 1126, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
